title: ligusticum wawrae
intro: Ligusticum wawrae, also known as Kika Koa or Chinese Lovage, is a plant species in the carrot family native to Hawaii and other Pacific islands. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant's health benefits include relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and soothing respiratory problems, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. Ligusticum wawrae can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional Hawaiian and Polynesian medicine.
